User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the MovaFin application, primarily for managing multi-tenancy and linking financial data. User authentication details are managed by an external system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

externalAuthId.description: Unique identifier from the external authentication system that corresponds to this user.

name.description: The display name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

AccountType.title: AccountType
AccountType.description: Defines a customizable type for financial accounts (e.g., 'Checking', 'Savings', 'Cash'). These types can be edited by individual users.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AccountType entity.

name.description: The name of the account type (e.g., 'Conta Corrente', 'Poupança').

description.description: A detailed description of the account type.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this custom account type. (Relationship: User 1:N AccountType)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the account type was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the account type was last updated.

Account.title: Account
Account.description: Represents a single financial account belonging to a user, such as a checking, savings, or cash account.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Account entity.

name.description: The user-defined name for this financial account (e.g., 'Minha Conta Principal').

description.description: An optional description for the account.

initialBalance.description: The initial monetary balance of the account when it was created.

currentBalance.description: The current monetary balance of the account.

currency.description: The currency code of the account (e.g., 'BRL', 'USD').

accountTypeId.description: Reference to the AccountType classifying this account. (Relationship: AccountType 1:N Account)

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this account. (Relationship: User 1:N Account)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the account was last updated.

TransactionCategory.title: TransactionCategory
TransactionCategory.description: Defines a customizable category for financial transactions (e.g., 'Food', 'Salary'). These categories can be edited by individual users.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TransactionCategory entity.

name.description: The name of the transaction category (e.g., 'Alimentação', 'Transporte').

description.description: A detailed description of the transaction category.

type.description: The general type of the category, either 'income' or 'expense'.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this custom transaction category. (Relationship: User 1:N TransactionCategory)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the transaction category was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the transaction category was last updated.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Records individual financial movements, categorized as either an income or an expense, linked to a specific account.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

amount.description: The monetary value of the transaction. Should always be stored as a positive value, with the 'type' field indicating if it's income or expense.

type.description: Indicates if the transaction is an 'income' or an 'expense'.

description.description: User-provided notes or observations about the transaction.

transactionDate.description: The date and time when the transaction occurred.

accountId.description: Reference to the Account to which this transaction belongs. (Relationship: Account 1:N Transaction)

transactionCategoryId.description: Reference to the TransactionCategory that classifies this transaction. (Relationship: TransactionCategory 1:N Transaction)

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this transaction. (Relationship: User 1:N Transaction)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the transaction record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the transaction record was last updated.

Attachment.title: Attachment
Attachment.description: Stores metadata about files attached to transactions, such as receipts or invoices.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Attachment entity.

filename.description: The original filename of the attached file.

url.description: The URL where the attached file is stored (e.g., in cloud storage).

mimeType.description: The MIME type of the attached file (e.g., 'image/jpeg', 'application/pdf').

fileSize.description: The size of the attached file in bytes.

transactionId.description: Reference to the Transaction this attachment belongs to. (Relationship: Transaction 1:N Attachment)

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this attachment. (Relationship: User 1:N Attachment)

uploadedAt.description: Timestamp when the attachment was uploaded.

definition.description: Top-level collection for user profiles. Each document ID corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID. This is the root for all user-specific data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Subcollection for user-defined account types. Each document includes the denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this account type.

params.name: accountTypeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the custom account type.

definition.description: Subcollection for user's financial accounts. Each document includes the denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this account.

params.name: accountId
params.description: The unique identifier for the financial account.

definition.description: Subcollection for user-defined transaction categories. Each document includes the denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this transaction category.

params.name: transactionCategoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the custom transaction category.

definition.description: Nested subcollection for transactions belonging to a specific account, owned by a user. Each document includes denormalized 'userId' and 'accountId' fields for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this transaction.

params.name: accountId
params.description: The unique identifier of the account to which this transaction belongs.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the transaction record.

definition.description: Deeply nested subcollection for attachments linked to a specific transaction, account, and user. Each document includes denormalized 'userId' and 'transactionId' fields for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this attachment.

params.name: accountId
params.description: The unique identifier of the account associated with the parent transaction.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the transaction to which this attachment belongs.

params.name: attachmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the attachment record.

definition.description: Top-level collection for administrative roles. The existence of a document with a user's UID in this collection grants them administrative privileges. This uses 'Existence over Content' for DBAC.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who has admin privileges.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and debuggable, strictly adhering to the core design principles and strategy mandates. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL) and QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
1.  
    **Path-Based Multi-Tenancy:** All user-specific data (Account Types, Accounts, Transaction Categories, Transactions, and Attachments) is nested directly under a user's document using the path `/users/{userId}/...`. This automatically enforces multi-tenancy, as `request.auth.uid` must match the `{userId}` in the path for any access, ensuring users only interact with their own data. This design is crucial for QAPs, as `list` operations on these collections (e.g., `/users/{request.auth.uid}/accounts`) are inherently secure and don't require server-side filtering after fetching. 
2.  
    **Denormalization of Ownership:** Every document within these user-specific subcollections (AccountType, Account, TransactionCategory, Transaction, Attachment) explicitly includes the `userId` field. This denormalization is the cornerstone of Authorization Independence. It eliminates the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls in security rules to check ownership against a parent document. For example, to verify ownership of an `Account`, the rule can directly check `resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid` in addition to `request.path[1] == request.auth.uid`. This makes rules atomic, performant, and easy to debug, while also enabling atomic write operations (transactions/batches) without breaking security checks.

**Structural Segregation & Access Modeling:**
*   User-owned entities are segregated into their own subcollections (e.g., `/users/{userId}/accounts`). Each subcollection maintains a homogeneous security posture: all documents within it are owned by the parent `userId` and have similar access patterns.
*   Global roles for administrators are handled via a dedicated top-level collection, `/roles_admin/{userId}`. This follows the "Existence over Content" principle, where the presence of a document with the user's UID signifies administrative privileges, enabling simple and efficient DBAC without custom claims.

**Data Clarity and Consistency:**
*   Wildcards are semantically named (e.g., `{userId}`, `{accountId}`, `{transactionId}`) for clarity.
*   Authorization fields are standardized (`userId`).
*   Explicit state modeling is used for transaction types (`type: 'income' | 'expense'`).

This architecture prioritizes security, scalability, and maintainability by leveraging Firestore's hierarchical nature for multi-tenancy and employing strategic denormalization to simplify security rules and enhance performance.
